welcome.subhead: Press the hotkey to start talking, press it again to stop — text appears instantly in whatever app you are using.

permissions.title_linux: Pick a microphone and an injection tool
permissions.summary_macos: macOS needs three permissions: Microphone (recording), Accessibility (writing transcribed text into the focused app), and Input Monitoring (listening for global hotkeys). Permission caches are bound to the running process — if you ticked the toggle in System Settings but it still shows as not granted, click Restart OpenSpeech in the top right so the new grants take effect.
permissions.summary_windows: Windows needs Microphone access for recording. If your target app runs as Administrator, OpenSpeech must run with the same privileges to inject text into it.
permissions.summary_linux: Linux has no permission prompt: pick a PipeWire / PulseAudio input device, and make sure ydotool / wtype (Wayland) or xdotool (X11) is installed.
permissions.tcc_hint: Toggle on but still says not granted? Remove OpenSpeech from this permission list in System Settings, add it back, then click Restart OpenSpeech.
permissions.footer_hint_ok: 2 steps left: sign in → try it
permissions.footer_hint_relaunch: Restart after granting · the next step opens automatically

mic.title: Microphone
mic.rationale: Records your voice and sends it to the recognition engine.
mic.rationale_linux: Linux has no permission prompt; just pick a PipeWire / PulseAudio input device.
mic.hint_macos: Grant in: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Microphone
mic.hint_windows: Grant in: Windows Settings → Privacy → Microphone

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.rationale: Simulates keyboard input to write recognized text into the focused field.
accessibility.hint_macos: Grant in: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility

input_monitoring.title: Input Monitoring
input_monitoring.rationale: Listens for global hotkeys (e.g. Fn / Ctrl+Win) to start recording while you are using other apps.
input_monitoring.hint_macos: Grant in: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Input Monitoring

inject_tools.rationale_windows: If the app you write into runs as Administrator (e.g. cmd), OpenSpeech needs the same privileges to inject text.
inject_tools.rationale_linux: Wayland needs ydotool / wtype, X11 needs xdotool. OpenSpeech detects this automatically.

login.title: Sign in to OpenLoaf
login.subtitle_openloaf: OpenSpeech is part of the OpenLoaf product family — one OpenLoaf account works across every product, with shared credits.
login.method_wechat_sub: Scan a QR code to sign in
login.status_polling: Waiting for you to finish in the browser…
login.advanced_expand: ↓ I have my own STT endpoint
login.advanced_desc: Configure a custom REST STT endpoint (OpenAI / Groq / Deepgram / self-hosted). Recommended for developers only — most users should sign in above.
login.advanced_cta: Skip sign-in and configure a custom endpoint
login.error_reset: Connection to the sign-in server was reset. Please try again.
login.error_timeout: Sign-in timed out. Please try again.
login.error_network: Cannot reach the sign-in server. Check your network and try again.

try_it.title: Try it once.
try_it.subhead: Press the hotkey below · say a sentence · press it again to stop

checklist.step1: Press the dictation hotkey to start
checklist.step3: Press the hotkey again to stop

mock_slices: Today the weather is great, so
Today the weather is great, so I opened OpenSpeech
Today the weather is great, so I opened OpenSpeech to try it out.
try_it.hotkey_hint: Press to start · press again to stop
try_it.completed_title: Setup complete — you are ready to go
try_it.completed_desc: In real use, text is written into whichever app has focus.

status.listening_primary: Press the hotkey again to stop and transcribe
status.error_primary: Something went wrong — check the log or retry
